text: Developers often spend a lot of time debugging, but a systematic approach makes it easier. The first step is identifying where the bug is—find the exact line of code or function causing the problem. Then, use debugging tools like breakpoints in your code editor to pause execution and examine variable values step by step. Alternatively, logging is useful: adding `print` or `console.log` statements to see what your program is doing over time. By testing one part at a time and noting the results, you can gradually isolate and fix the issue without guessing. This method saves time and frustration.
